Paragraph

Any open set \(G\) of \(\bbR\) is a union of at most countably many open intervals \((c_{i}, d_{i})\text{.}\) For any \(c\in C[a, b]\) and any open subset \(G\) of \([a, b]\text{,}\) the integrals \(\int_{G} c(x)\, dx\) and \(\int_{G} |c(x)|^{p}\, dx\) have clearly defined meanings as the (absolutely convergent) sum of the integrals on each constitutive open intervals, with \(\int_{G} 1\, dx=|G|\text{,}\) the length of \(G\) .
in-context
